task_completion.description: Investigate alerts and security events in the Wazuh dashboard
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser open to the Wazuh dashboard (https://localhost) showing security events, alerts, or the modules interface.

task_completion.description: Create a correlation rule with frequency, timeframe, if_matched_sid, and level >= 13
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or the Wazuh UI showing an XML rule definition containing the attributes 'frequency', 'timeframe', 'if_matched_sid', and a 'level' attribute set to 13 or higher.

task_completion.description: Configure a Wazuh active response in ossec.conf triggered by the new rule
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing the 'ossec.conf' file with an '<active-response>' block being added or modified, referencing the newly created rule ID.

task_completion.description: Create the incident report at the specified Desktop path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open to '/home/ga/Desktop/incident_report.txt' or a file manager showing 'incident_report.txt' on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Document the investigation details in the report (minimum 300 characters)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'incident_report.txt' are visible and contain substantial text (several paragraphs) detailing rule IDs, correlation patterns, thresholds, and the automated response configuration.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Wazuh web interface to investigate alerts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Wazuh GUI loaded in a browser, rather than the agent just guessing rule IDs or skipping the investigation step entirely.

integrity.description: The agent modified the actual Wazuh configuration files rather than mock files.
integrity.visual_evidence: File paths for the rule creation and ossec.conf edits point to actual system directories (e.g., /var/ossec/etc/) or are done through the official Wazuh UI.

integrity.description: The agent wrote a meaningful incident report rather than pasting the prompt or gibberish.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in incident_report.txt reads like a genuine SOC investigation report and does not simply copy-paste the task instructions to meet the character limit.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The minimum required rule level for the correlation rule is 13. The incident report must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/Desktop/incident_report.txt' and contain at least 300 characters. The underlying events being investigated are synthetically generated failed SSH and sudo attempts.
